# Data Directory — KGE Datasets & Crawler Output

This folder contains all data files generated by the pipeline.

## Files Generated by the Pipeline

### Web Crawler Output
| File | Generated by | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `crawled_pages.jsonl` | `src/crawl/crawler.py` | Raw crawled pages: `{url, text, word_count, timestamp, title, domain}` |

### NER + Triple Extraction
| File | Generated by | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `entities.csv` | `src/ie/ner_extractor.py` | Columns: `entity, type, source_url, count` |
| `triples.csv` | `src/ie/ner_extractor.py` | Columns: `subject, predicate, object, source_url` |

### KGE Dataset (Knowledge Graph Embedding)
| File | Generated by | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `entity2id.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Entity name → integer ID mapping. First line is count. |
| `relation2id.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Relation name → integer ID mapping. First line is count. |
| `train.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Training triples in tab-separated `head_id\trelation_id\ttail_id` format |
| `valid.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Validation triples (10% of dataset) |
| `test.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Test triples (10% of dataset) |
| `train_readable.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Human-readable training triples: `head\trelation\ttail` |
| `valid_readable.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Human-readable validation triples |
| `test_readable.txt` | `src/kge/kge_prep.py` | Human-readable test triples |

## File Formats

### entity2id.txt / relation2id.txt
```
N                   ← number of entities/relations
entity_name\tid     ← tab-separated pairs
...
```

Example:
```
25
Yann_LeCun    0
Geoffrey_Hinton    1
...
```

### train.txt / valid.txt / test.txt
```
head_id\trelation_id\ttail_id
```
Integer IDs corresponding to entity2id.txt and relation2id.txt.

### crawled_pages.jsonl
Each line is a JSON object:
```json
{
  "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yann_LeCun",
  "text": "Yann LeCun is a French computer scientist...",
  "word_count": 1234,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "title": "Yann LeCun",
  "domain": "en.wikipedia.org"
}
```

### entities.csv
```
entity,type,source_url,count
Yann LeCun,PERSON,https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yann_LeCun,5
MIT,ORG,https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geoffrey_Hinton,3
...
```

### triples.csv
```
subject,predicate,object,source_url
Yann LeCun,work,Meta AI,https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yann_LeCun
Geoffrey Hinton,receive,Turing Award,https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geoffrey_Hinton
...
```

## Dataset Statistics (expected after full pipeline run)

After running all pipeline steps:

| Metric | Expected value |
|--------|---------------|
| Crawled pages | 30-45 |
| Extracted entities | 200-500 |
| Extracted triples | 100-300 |
| KGE entities | 50-100 |
| KGE relations | 8-15 |
| Train triples | ~80 |
| Valid triples | ~10 |
| Test triples | ~10 |

Note: If the crawler has not been run, `kge_prep.py` uses the
built-in sample triples from `kg_artifacts/sample_kg.ttl`.

## Notes

- Files in this directory are **gitignored** (large/generated)
- To regenerate from scratch, run the pipeline in order (see README.md)
- The KGE files use integer IDs for efficiency with PyKEEN
- The `*_readable.txt` files use entity names for interpretability
